---
title: "Publications"
---

## Peer-Reviewed Journal Articles

- Kang, Bora. “Performative Exhaustion: Legibility, Complicity, and Limit in *A Gesture Life*.” *MELUS* 51.3 (forthcoming Jan. 2027). (Accepted Jun. 2026).
- Kang, Bora. “‘Whoever You Are to Yourself’: Conditional Hospitality and Affective Asymmetry in Ali Smith’s *Winter*.” *Journal of English Studies in Korea (JESK)* 50 (2026): 5–31. [https://doi.org/10.46562/jesk.50.1](https://doi.org/10.46562/jesk.50.1)
- Kang, Bora. “From National Trauma to Transnational Testimony: Comfort Women Cinema and the Politics of Postmemory.” *The Journal of Modern British & American Language & Literature* 44.2 (2026): 25–46. [https://doi.org/10.21084/jmball.2026.44.2.25](https://doi.org/10.21084/jmball.2026.44.2.25)
- Kang, Bora. “The Chain of Refiguration: Reading Woolf-Cunningham-Daldry through Ricoeur’s Triple Mimesis.” *The Korean Association of Literature and Film* 27.1 (2026): 7–38. [https://doi.org/10.36114/JLF.2026.4.27.1.7](https://doi.org/10.36114/JLF.2026.4.27.1.7)
- Kang, Bora and Sara Lee. “The Direction of Character Education in College: focused on the current character education programs in domestic and foreign universities.” *Journal of Women’s Studies* 28 (2013): 34–69.

---

## Manuscripts Under Review & In Progress

### Book Projects
- Kang, Bora. *Affective Drift and Englishness from Woolf to Brexit: Literature, Film, and Media*. (Monograph proposal and sample chapters submitted, at editor's invitation, Edinburgh University Press, Jul. 2026).
- Kang, Bora, and Liyang Dong, eds. *Women’s Writing and Storytelling as Resilience in Transnational Narratives*. (Vernon Press, edited collection proposal under review; contributors selected, sample chapters in progress).

### Journal Articles & Volume Chapters
- Kang, Bora. “Heritage Aesthetics as Streaming’s Emotional Template.” (Revise & Resubmit invited at *Television and New Media*, Mar. 2026; revised manuscript resubmitted, decision pending).
- Kang, Bora. “Feeling Brexit: Drift and Saturation in Ali Smith’s *Autumn* and *Winter*.” (Revise & Resubmit invited at *Contemporary Women’s Writing*, Jul. 2026).
- Kang, Bora. “Dignity as Discipline: Affective Loops and Post-Imperial Englishness in Ishiguro’s *The Remains of the Day*.” (Under review at *Modern Fiction Studies*).
- Kang, Bora. “Aesthetic Containment: Formalist Aesthetics as Affective Governance in D. H. Lawrence’s *Women in Love*.” (Under review at *Textual Practice*).
- Kang, Bora. “After Speech: Narrative Labor and Postcolonial Englishness in Abdulrazak Gurnah’s *By the Sea*.” (Under review at *ARIEL: A Review of International English Literature*).
- Kang, Bora. “Slightly Out of Tune: Affective Drift and Weird Modernism in Virginia Woolf’s *Mrs. Dalloway*.” (Under review at *Studies in the Novel*).
- Kang, Bora. “Quiet Acts of Belonging: Emotional Endurance and Britishness in Asian Diasporic Women’s Narratives.” (Invited book chapter in preparation for *Women’s Writing and Storytelling as Resilience in Transnational Narratives*, Vernon Press).
